pages.full_text:
So we back-tracked about 12 miles to __Sacé__, * * a drive-in campsite in a village, pretty beech hedges, choose your own plot, & the warden comes to collect money at 7, 6 & 10 pm.
__Monday__
We stopped off for coffee in a forest with lots of horseflies. We drove on to __Baugé__, where
pages.notes: Page has a glued-in printed French bakery card ('Le Moulin de Brimeux — Une petite entreprise indépendante au service des Artisans-Boulangers / Vous aussi, Faites confiance à la Qualité Traditionnelle / de votre Artisan Boulanger-Pâtissier') occupying most of the upper half; handwritten text begins below. The asterisks after 'Sacé' may be campsite rating symbols. Text continues on the right page. No handwritten page number visible. The volume covers 2003–2019; no year is legible from this page alone — likely France trip, year undetermined.

pages.full_text:
Venetia Grommett's mother puts on operas. The campsite was municipal & had "Post" in the [?name] * * * ?. The washing machine didn't work at first. Irish people tried to help me. There was a super pool next door - one of the nicest I've swum in - hugely wide, so that old ladies can sedately crawl down the middle without being bombed by the youth congregating at the sides.
At 4.40 we set off to walk to the Opera - Richard the Lionheart, Handel. It was in a hired [?open-air] theatre & was the hottest I've every been in my life - sitting streaming with sweat. In the middle we had supper on the grass - delicious paniers arrived. Sweat poured down. Fabulous, fabulous duets.
pages.notes: Page numbered 36 at top-right. Top portion has a printed/illustrated card continuing from the left page (Le Moulin de Brimeux imagery with green tree illustration visible). 'Venetia Grommett' — surname could be 'Grommett', 'Grommit', or similar; difficult to read with certainty. 'paniers' may be 'paniers' (French for baskets/hampers) or could be 'paniers' meaning picnic baskets. 'Richard the Lionheart' opera by Handel performed at Baugé is consistent with the Baugé area in the Loire valley. 'Post' is underlined and followed by asterisks — appears to be a campsite classification. The word after 'the' before the asterisks is hard to read — could be 'name' or the campsite name itself. 'every' appears to be the writer's spelling of 'ever'. No year visible on this page.
